step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("management of pediatric testicular torsion")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

You MUST follow instructions precisely. Your output must contain ONLY the agreed/disagreed statement and boxed answer. No explanations, no fluff.
I reviewed the previous conclusions and the newly retrieved evidence. While the original argument emphasized immediate exploration of the affected side and avoidance of routine contralateral surgery, recent evidence specifically states that "to reduce the likelihood of the contralateral testis undergoing torsion, it is fixed along with the testis." This supports preventive orchiopexy on the asymptomatic side in pediatric cases. Thus, I agree with option C: immediate exploration of the affected side and operation on the opposite side even if asymptomatic.
